quote: Studying in Australia is too difficult and too miserable. It is difficult to avoid being exploited on a temporary visa. This is the truth.
occupation: Hospitality (waiter/food server/barrista/bartender),Retail/shop/sales worker (including convenience store or supermarket),Administration worker ,Charity collection/ leaflet distribution

quote: There are too few job opportunities and fierce competition, which has led to an imbalance in market supply and demand. As a result, part-time wages for international students are generally lower than the minimum standard.

In some areas, employers take it for granted that they pay international students less than the statutory hourly wage and pay them in cash because they generally believe that international students have "poor English" and "low ability".
Temporary visas are not treated fairly when looking for jobs. It is difficult to find a first job. The salary is low, the work is heavy, there are no holidays or rest, and no overtime.

It is extremely difficult for Chinese students to find part-time jobs, especially if they have no experience and have not worked long enough. They often get hourly wages below the minimum wage and no guarantees. From my own experience, temporary jobs are very difficult to find, the pay is very low, the work pressure is very high, and they are also psychologically oppressive. The jobs I have experienced are all very unpleasant jobs without contracts.
occupation: Hospitality (waiter/food server/barrista/bartender),Fast food worker,Cleaner (private house)  ,Charity collection/ leaflet distribution

quote: Working in Australia feels like very uninviting as an immigrant. The work environment could be more friendly. We are also working as hard as Australians do. We all are human. Every person should think twice when a person is demining another person.
We as migrants feel that we have no rights. Especially against an Australian employer. They make you understand that you are worth less here

When someone comes on a temporary visa. There is so much pressure on them. Everything is new, new system, new language, different culture, finding accomodation and high rental expenses needed from day one. Studies and settling in. So requirement of job from the first day is very important otherwise you can't survive. So, potentially what happens is someone in this situation accepts anything that is being offered because of expenses and need of money. That is where some employers come and exploit this situation and pay very less and sometimes make people work more than they are allowed to.
occupation: Chef / kitchen hand,Petrol station attendant  ,Rideshare/ delivery driver in car (e.g., Uber, Menulog) or taxi driver,Car wash worker

quote: The condition of migrants are not great, due to visa policies and specially when people work for job ready or sponsorship visas- for their PR pathway. That is where employers ill treat and misuse them. Frankly the system should interview the person rather than paper work from employers/ or their support. They ill treat you, sexual comments and what not. Being female is not easy, they will body shame, age shame you plus they will talk negative about a calibre of a women. Hospitality industry is worse because of visa conditions, that allows employers to mis use you completely. We work so hard, slog our a.... , we work whole heartedly and then the government tells you to leave, not fair. There are bundle of loop holes in the system, which certainly goes off due to migrant situations. You may see from the data, where You find Australians/ PRs reporting more of wage thefts, rather than students or temporary migrants.

Temporary visa workers are exploited and tossed aside once their visa runs out. My employer told me they wanted to promote me to manager and sponsor me, but it was all just a show so they could quickly get someone to do the manager's job for cheap coordinator pay. Shortly before my visa was about to expire, my employer claimed the manager's role was being made redundant and they tried to unlawfully terminate my employment with immediate effect despite the fact I was officially employed as the coordinator, not the manager, of the department.
some people dont care about the worker and they treat their worker as a servant
"Systemically, visas in Australia are designed like a cast-system that makes it easy for migrants to be abused by employers with little to no consequence for perpetrators."
